title: Implement wrapup.md parser with agent-based step decomposition
description:
Create internal/wrapup/parse.go with:

1. ParseWrapupFile(tickDir string) (string, error) — reads .tick/wrapup.md, returns contents or empty string if not found.

2. ParseWrapupSteps(ctx context.Context, agent agent.Agent, content string, opts agent.RunOpts) ([]WrapupStep, error) — sends freeform wrapup.md content to agent with a decomposition prompt, parses JSON array response into []WrapupStep. Retry once with stricter prompt on invalid JSON.

3. LoadCachedSteps(logsDir string, epicID string) ([]WrapupStep, bool) — loads from .tick/logs/wrapup-steps/<epic-id>.json if exists.

4. CacheSteps(logsDir string, epicID string, steps []WrapupStep) error — writes parsed steps to cache.

Types:
  type WrapupStep struct {
      Title  string `json:"title"`
      Prompt string `json:"prompt"`
      Verify string `json:"verify"`
  }

The parsing prompt asks the agent to extract discrete, independently completable steps with title, full prompt, and verify (what done looks like). No structural requirements on wrapup.md — it can be prose, bullets, numbered lists, anything.

Files: internal/wrapup/parse.go, internal/wrapup/parse_test.go
Tests: mock agent returning valid JSON, malformed JSON retry, empty content, cache hit/miss.
closed_reason: Created internal/wrapup/parse.go with ParseWrapupFile (reads .tick/wrapup.md), ParseWrapupSteps (agent-based decomposition with JSON retry), LoadCachedSteps/CacheSteps (cache in .tick/logs/wrapup-steps/). Reused WrapupStep type from step_prompt.go. Added parse_test.go with 12 tests covering valid/malformed JSON, retry, empty content, cache hit/miss, and JSON extraction.
